dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 6904 IH: Veterans Readiness and Employment Improvement and Accountability Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mr. Van Orden introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Veterans' Affairs

form.official-title: To amend title 38, United States Code, to make certain improvements to rehabilitation programs for veterans with service-connected disabilities, to establish a new bar to certain benefits under laws administered by the Secretary of Veterans Affairs, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Veterans Readiness and Employment Improvement and Accountability Act.

section.header: Bar to certain veterans benefits: conviction of assaulting, resisting, or impeding an officer or employee of the Department of Veterans Affairs

subsection.text: Section 5303(a) of title 38, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: by inserting (1) before The discharge; and
by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

subparagraph.text: The Secretary may bar a person described in subparagraph (B) from benefits under chapter 30, 31, 33, 35, or 36 of this title.
A person described in this subparagraph is a person convicted under section 111 of title 18 for an offense against an officer or employee of the Department.

subsection.text: The amendments made by this section shall apply to a conviction under section 111 of title 18 for an offense against an officer or employee of the Department of Veterans Affairs that occurs on or after the date of the enactment of this Act.

section.header: Periods of eligibility: additional vocational rehabilitation programs
section.text: Section 3103(c)(2) of title 38, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking , or (B) and inserting , (B); and

by striking ; or and inserting , or (C) the veteran has not obtained employment (i) in the occupation for which such veteran had been so trained (ii) during the one-year period after the end of such training; or.

section.header: Scope of services and assistance for a rehabilitation program: approval of the Secretary required for certain payments

subsection.text: Section 3104(a) of title 38, United States Code, is amended, in paragraph (7), by adding at the end the following new subparagraph:

subparagraph.text: A payment for equipment under subparagraph (A) that exceeds $5,000 shall require the approval of the Secretary.

subsection.text: Not later than one year after the date of the enactment of this Act, and annually thereafter for five years, the Secretary of Veterans Affairs shall submit to the Committees on Veterans’ Affairs of the Senate and House of Representatives a report of payments approved under subparagraph (C) of such paragraph, as added by this section, during the year preceding the date of such report. In such a report, the Secretary shall identify, with regard to each such payment—

paragraph.text: the reason for the purchase; and
the total equipment purchased for the rehabilitation program of the veteran.

section.header: Maximum amount for a rehabilitation program; automatic adjustment
section.text: Section 3104 of title 38, United States Code, is amended, in subsection (b)—

paragraph.text: by inserting (1) before A rehabilitation program;
by designating the second and third sentences, collectively, as paragraph (2) of such subsection and adjusting the margins accordingly; and
by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

subparagraph.text: Subject to subparagraph (B), the amount of Federal funds paid for a rehabilitation program may not exceed $250,000.

Effective October 1, 2026, and each year thereafter, the Secretary shall increase the dollar amount in effect under paragraph (A), by a percentage equal to the most recent percentage increase under section 3015(h) of this title.

section.header: Vocational rehabilitation specialist: authority to redevelop individualized vocational rehabilitation plan; definition

subsection.text: Section 3106(f) of such title is amended by inserting (who may be a vocational rehabilitation specialist) after employee.
Section 3101 of title 38, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following new paragraph:

paragraph.text: The term vocational rehabilitation specialist means—

subparagraph.text: a vocational rehabilitation specialist in the Veteran Readiness and Employment division of the Veterans Benefits Administration; or
a counseling psychologist of the Department performing the duties of a vocational rehabilitation specialist.

section.header: Subsistence allowance for a veteran entitled to a rehabilitation program and Post-9/11 educational assistance: basis of location
section.text: Section 3108(b)(4) of title 38, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking the institution providing the rehabilitation program concerned. and inserting an em dash; and
by adding at the end the following new subparagraphs:

subparagraph.text: the institution providing the rehabilitation program concerned; or
the residence of the veteran if such residence is more than 25 miles from such institution.

section.text: Section 3117 of title 38, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following new subsection:

subsection.text: To carry out this section, the Secretary shall, to the extent practicable, employ an employment counselor at each regional office of the Department.

section.header: Ineligibility of a veteran in a vocational rehabilitation program for disability compensation on the basis of a disability rated as total on the basis of individual unemployability

subsection.text: Chapter 31 of title 38, United States Code, is amended by adding at the end the following new section:

section.header: Ineligibility of a veteran in a vocational rehabilitation program for compensation for a disability rated as total on the basis of individual unemployability
section.text: A veteran participating in a vocational rehabilitation program may not receive disability compensation under chapter 11 of this title for a disability rated as total on the basis of individual unemployability.

subsection.text: The table of sections at the beginning of such chapter is amended by adding at the end the following new item:

toc.toc-entry: 3123. Ineligibility of a veteran in a vocational rehabilitation program for compensation for a disability rated as total on the basis of individual unemployability.

subsection.text: Section 3101 of title 38, United States Code, is amended by redesignating the paragraph (10) relating to the term emergency situation as paragraph (1).
Section 3103 of title 38, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: in the second subsection (h), by inserting in after participating;
by redesignating such second subsection (h), as amended, as subsection (i); and
in subsection (a), by striking or (h) and inserting (h) or (i).
